phrases.intent_en: Ask where the Mona Lisa is
phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, où se trouve La Joconde ?
phrases.pronunciation: ex-koo-ZAY mwah, oo suh TROOV lah zhoh-KOHND
phrases.cultural_note: The French call the Mona Lisa 'La Joconde'. Follow the signs for 'La Joconde' in the Denon wing. Expect large crowds — visit early morning or Wednesday/Friday evenings.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how to get to the Venus de Milo
phrases.text_local: Pour aller à la Vénus de Milo, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poor ah-LAY ah lah vay-NOOS duh MEE-loh, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: The Venus de Milo is on the ground floor of the Sully wing. It's less crowded than the Mona Lisa and well worth the visit.

phrases.intent_en: Ask which wing to visit for Egyptian antiquities
phrases.text_local: Les antiquités égyptiennes sont dans quelle aile ?
phrases.pronunciation: layz ahn-tee-kee-TAY ay-zheep-SYEHN sohn dahn kel EL
phrases.cultural_note: The Egyptian collection is in the Sully wing. Don't miss the Great Sphinx and the Seated Scribe — the Louvre has one of the world's best Egyptian collections.

phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'il y a un audioguide en anglais ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess keel ee ah uhn oh-dee-oh-GHEED ahn ahn-GLEH
phrases.cultural_note: The Louvre offers a Nintendo 3DS audio guide in several languages. You can also download the free Louvre app before your visit.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the Winged Victory of Samothrace
phrases.text_local: Où est la Victoire de Samothrace ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo eh lah veek-TWAHR duh sah-moh-TRAHS
phrases.cultural_note: The Winged Victory sits atop the grand Daru staircase in the Denon wing. It's one of the most dramatic presentations of any artwork in the world.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there's a faster entrance
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il une entrée avec moins d'attente ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL oon ahn-TRAY ah-VEK mwehn dah-TAHNT
phrases.cultural_note: Skip the pyramid line by entering through the Carrousel du Louvre (underground mall), the Passage Richelieu, or the Porte des Lions. Museum Pass holders have a separate fast lane.
